title: Cleo Kestrel
content:
Cleo Kestrel (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as head coach of the Glimmerford Argonauts when they won the Lakefront Championship Series in 2012.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the Lakefront Championship Series, Cleo Kestrel remained with the Glimmerford Argonauts for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Playing style.

As head coach, Cleo Kestrel preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Glimmerford Argonauts switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Legacy.

Historians of the Glimmerford Argonauts cite Cleo Kestrel's 2012 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Glimmerford Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Personal life.

Cleo Kestrel retired to Glimmerford and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 2014.

Awards and honors.

Following the Lakefront Championship Series, Cleo Kestrel received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Professional career.

Cleo Kestrel joined the Glimmerford Argonauts and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-2007s. By 2011, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Early life and college career.

Cleo Kestrel played college basketball in Glimmerford before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as head coach.
